questions.question_html: The population of Sundarnagar was 2,35,471 in the year 1991. In the year 2001, it was found to be increased by 72,958. What was the population of the city in 2001?
questions.solution_html:
Population of the city in 2001
= Population of the city in 1991 + Increase in population
= 2,35,471 + 72,958
Now, {tex}\begin{array}{r} {235471} \\ {+72958} \\ \hline 308429 \end{array}{/tex}
Therefore, Population of the city in 2001 was 3,08,429.

questions.question_html: In one state, the number of bicycles sold in the year 2002-2003 was 7,43,000. In the year 2003-2004, the number of bicycles sold was 8,00,100. In which year were more bicycles sold? and how many more?
questions.solution_html:
Clearly, 8,00,100 is more than 7,43,000. So, in that state, more bicycles were sold in the year 2003-2004 than in 2002-2003.
Now, 800100 - 743000 = 57100
Therefore, 57,100 more bicycles were sold in the year 2003-2004.

questions.question_html: The town newspaper is published every day. One copy has 12 pages. Every day 11,980 copies are printed. How many total pages are printed every day?
questions.solution_html:
Each copy has 12 pages.
Hence, 11,980 copies will have 12 {tex}\times{/tex} 11,980 pages. 
Now, 

Therefore, Everyday 1,43,760 pages are printed.

questions.question_html: The number of sheets of paper available for making notebooks is 75,000. Each sheet makes 8 pages of a notebook. Each notebook contains 200 pages. How many notebooks can be made from the paper available?
questions.solution_html:
Given: Each sheet makes 8 pages.
Hence, 75,000 sheets make 8 {tex}\times{/tex} 75,000 pages
Now, 75000 {tex}\times{/tex} 8 = 600000
Thus, 6,00,000 pages are available for making notebooks. Now, 200 pages make 1 notebook.
Hence, 6,00,000 pages make 6,00,000 {tex}\div{/tex} 200 notebooks.
Now, 

Therefore, 3,000 notebooks can be made.

questions.question_html: 1 million = ________ lakh
1 lakh = ________ ten thousand.
1 crore =  ________ ten lakh (million).
1 crore = ________ million
1 million =  ________ hundred thousand

Place comma correctly and write the numeral:
Seventy three lakh seventy five thousand three hundred seven.
questions.solution_html: In numerals Seventy three lakh seventy five thousand three hundred seven is 73,75,307

questions.question_html:
Place comma correctly and write the numeral:
Nine crore five lakh forty one.
questions.solution_html: In numerals Nine crore five lakh forty one is 9,05,00,041

questions.question_html:
Place comma correctly and write the numeral:
Seven crore fifty two lakh twenty one thousand three hundred two.
questions.solution_html: In numerals, Seven crore fifty two lakh twenty one thousand three hundred two is 7,52,21,302

questions.question_html:
Place comma correctly and write the numeral:
Fifty eight million four hundred twenty three thousand two hundred two.
questions.solution_html: In numerals, Fifty eight million four hundred twenty three thousand two hundred two is 58,423,202

questions.question_html:
Place comma correctly and write the numeral:
Twenty three lakh thirty thousand ten.
questions.solution_html: In numerals, Twenty three lakh thirty thousand ten is 23,30,010

questions.question_html:
Insert commas suitably and write the names according to Indian System of Numeration:
87595762
questions.solution_html:
According to the Indian Numeral system, each digit has values according to their place.
Let’s simplify 87595762, we have,
= 8, 0000,000 + 70, 00,000 + 5, 00,000 + 90,000 + 5, 000 + 700 + 60 + 2
= 8, 0000,000 + 70, 00,000 + 5, 00,000 + 90,000 + 5, 000 + 700 + sixty two
= 8, 0000,000 + 70, 00,000 + 5, 00,000 + 90,000 + 5, 000 + seven hundred sixty two
= 8, 0000,000 + 70, 00,000 + 5, 00,000 + 90,000 + five thousand seven hundred sixty-two
= 8, 0000,000 + 70, 00,000 + 5, 00,000 + ninty five thousand seven hundred sixty two
= 8, 0000,000 + 70, 00,000 + five lakh ninety five thousand seven hundred sixty two
= 8, 0000,000 + seventy-five lakh ninety-five thousand seven hundred sixty two
= Eight crore seventy-five lakh ninety-five thousand seven hundred sixty-two.

Insert comma suitably and write the name according to International System of Numeration:

questions.solution_html: Here 48049831 can be written as 48,049,831 = Forty-eight million forty-nine thousand eight hundred thirty-one.

questions.question_html: A book exhibition was held for four days in a school. The number of tickets sold at the counter on the first, second, third and final days was respectively 1094, 1812, 2050 and 2751. Find the total number of tickets sold on all the four days.
questions.solution_html:
Number of tickets sold on the first day = 1094
Number of tickets sold on the second day = 1812
Number of tickets sold on the third day = 2050
Number of tickets sold on the final day = 2751
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Total number of tickets sold on all the four days = 1094 + 1812 + 2050 + 2751 = 7707

questions.question_html: Shekhar is a famous cricket player. He has so far scored 6,980 runs in test matches. He wishes to complete 10,000 runs. How many more runs does he need?
questions.solution_html:
Runs scored so far = 6,980
Runs wished to be scored = 10,000
∴ Runs needed more = 10,000 – 6,980 = 3,020.

questions.question_html: In an election, the successful candidate registered 5,77,500 votes and his nearest rival secured 3,48,700 votes. By what margin did the successful candidate win the election?
questions.solution_html:
Votes registered by the successful candidate = 5,77,500
Votes secured by the nearest rival = 3,48,700
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Margin by which the successful candidate won the election
= 5,77,500 – 3,48,700 = 2,28,800

questions.question_html: Kirti Bookstore sold books worth ₹ 2,85,891 in the first week of June and books worth ₹ 4,00,768 in the second week of the month. How much was the sale for the two weeks together? In which week was the sale greater and by how much?
questions.solution_html:
Sale of books in the first week = Rs.2,85,891.
Sale of books in the second week = Rs.4,00,768
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Sale for the two weeks together = Rs.2,85,891 + Rs.4,00,768 = Rs.6,86,659.
The sale was greater in the second week by Rs. 4,00,768 – Rs.2,85,891 i.e., by Rs.1,14,877.

questions.question_html: Find the difference between the greatest and the least 5-digit number that can be written using the digits 6, 2, 7, 4, 3 each only once.
questions.solution_html:
Greatest number that can be written using the digits 6, 2, 7, 4, 3 each only once = 76,432.
Least number that can be written using the digits 6, 2, 7, 4, 3 each only once = 23,467.
∴ Difference between the greatest and least numbers that can be written using the digits 6, 2, 7, 4, 3 each only once
= 76,432 – 23,467 = 52,965.

questions.question_html: A machine, on an average, manufactures 2,825 screws a day. How many screws did it produce in the month of January, 2006?
questions.solution_html:
Number of screws manufactured by the machine a day on an average = 2,825.
Number of days in the month of January, 2006 = 31
∴ Number of screws produced by the machine in the month of January, 2006 = 2,825 × 31 = 87,575

questions.question_html: A merchant had ₹ 78,592 with her. She placed an order for purchasing 40 radio sets at ₹ 1200 each. How much money will remain with her after the purchase?
questions.solution_html:
Money which the merchant had = Rs.78,592
Cost of 40 radio sets at Rs.1200 each = Rs.1200 × 40 = Rs.48,000
∴ Money that will remain with the merchant after the purchase = Rs. 78,592 – Rs.48,000 = Rs.30,592.

questions.question_html:
A student multiplied 7236 by 65 instead of multiplying by 56. How much was his answer greater than the correct answer?
(Hint: Do you need to do both the multiplications?)
questions.solution_html:
Wrong answer was greater than the correct answer by
= 7236 {tex}\times{/tex} 65 – 7236 {tex}\times{/tex} 56
= 7236 {tex}\times{/tex} (65 – 56)
= 7236 {tex}\times{/tex} 9 = 65,124

questions.question_html:
To stitch a shirt 2m 15cm cloth is needed. Out of 40m cloth, how many shirts can be stitched and how much cloth will remain?
(Hint: convert data in cm.)
questions.solution_html:
2m 15cm = 2m + 15cm = 2{tex}\times{/tex}100cm + 15cm = 200cm + 15cm = 215cm
40m = 40{tex}\times{/tex}100cm = 4000cm.

Total length of cloth = 4000cm

Cloth needed for one shirt = 215 cm

No. Of shirts can be stitched = 4000cm{tex}\div{/tex}215cm 

Hence, 18 shirts can be stitched and 130 cm, i.e. 1m 30cm cloth will remain.

questions.question_html: Medicine is packed in boxes, each weighing 4kg 500g. How many such boxes can be loaded in a van which cannot carry beyond 800 kg?
questions.solution_html:
4kg 500g = 4kg + 500g = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 1,000g + 500g
= 4,000g + 500g = 4,500g
800kg = 800 {tex}\times{/tex} 1,000g = 8,00,000g.
Weight the van can carry = 8,00,000g
Weight of one box = 4,500g
No. of boxes that can be loaded = 8,00,000g {tex}\div{/tex} 4,500g


Hence, 177 such boxes can be loaded.

questions.question_html: The distance between the school and a student’s house is 1 km 875 m. Every day she walks both ways. Find the total distance covered by her in six days.
questions.solution_html:
1 km 875 m = 1 km + 875m = 1 {tex}\times{/tex} 1,000m + 875m
= 1,000m + 875m = 1875m
Distance covered by her in a day by walking both ways between school and home
= 1,875 {tex}\times{/tex} 2m = 3,750m
Working days in a week = 6
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Total distance covered by her in six days by walking both ways between school and home = 3,750 {tex}\times{/tex} 6m = 22,500m
= 22,000m + 500m = {tex}\frac{22000}{1000} \mathrm{km}{/tex} + 500m = 22km + 500m
= 22km 500m

questions.question_html: A vessel has 4 litres and 500ml of curd. In how many glasses, each of 25ml capacity, can it be filled?
questions.solution_html:
4l 500 ml = 4l + 500ml = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 1,000ml + 500ml
= 4,000 ml + 500ml = 4,500 ml
Total quantity of curd = 4,500 ml
Capacity of 1 glass = 25 ml
No. of glasses that can be filled = 4,500 ml ÷ 25 ml 

Hence, it can be filled in 180 glasses.
